channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sharp decline in gold prices as global rates outlook and war-driven market pressures weigh on demand
item.description: Gold prices in Pakistan saw a major drop on Monday, falling by Rs43,600 per tola to Rs447,762 for 24-karat gold. This decline follows a sharp downturn in international markets, where gold prices have ...

item.title: NYSC Local Government Inspector arraigned over female corper’s death
item.description: The Local Government Inspector (LGI) of NYSC, Abeokuta South Local Government Area, Ogun State, Abbas Olalekan, don face Isabo Magistrate Court in Abeokuta over alleged conspiracy and attempt to procu...

item.title: Raphael Tuju reported missing after his car is found abandoned in Karen, opposition calls for urgent investigations
item.description: Former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju has been reported missing in Nairobi after his vehicle was discovered abandoned in the affluent Karen area, with his phone switched off and no confirmed sightings...

item.title: Raphael Tuju arrested at Karen Police Station after presenting himself over alleged abduction claims
item.description: Former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju was arrested shortly after reappearing following a mysterious 24-hour disappearance that had sparked public concern. Tuju resurfaced alongside several opposition ...

item.title: Rising Costs Force UK Middle-Income Families to Reduce Leisure Spending
item.description: Many middle-income families in the UK are finding it increasingly difficult to afford outings and restaurant meals as the cost of living continues to rise. The BBC’s Panorama programme followed two fa...

item.title: Raphael Tuju Recounts Events Leading to His Brief Disappearance in Karen
item.description: Former Cabinet Secretary Raphael Tuju went missing on Saturday, March 22, but reappeared the following day at his Karen home. His family raised the alarm after being unable to reach him and identifyin...

item.title: Japan Partners with African Countries to Drive Innovation and Development
item.description: The Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA) continues to strengthen its partnership with African nations by promoting human security and sustainable growth. Dr TANAKA Akihiko, JICA President, hi...

item.title: Zanzibar and United States strengthen cooperation in health, climate and trade sectors
item.description: Zanzibar President and Chairman of the Revolutionary Council, Dr Hussein Ali Mwinyi, met with the Acting United States Ambassador to Tanzania, Andrew Lentz, in talks aimed at strengthening cooperation...

item.title: Communities in B.C. and Alberta Face Tragedy Yet Celebrate as Kraft Hockeyville Finalists
item.description: The 2026 Kraft Hockeyville competition has narrowed down to two resilient Canadian communities: Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, and Taber, Alberta. Both towns have endured significant hardship in rec...

item.title: CWC says majority of adolescent pregnancies involve adult men, highlights poverty and child protection issues
item.description: Ayon sa Council for the Welfare of Children (CWC), karamihan ng pagbubuntis ng mga adolescent girls sa Pilipinas ay kinasasangkutan ng mga adult men. Ipinaliwanag ni CWC Executive Director Angelo Tapa...

item.title: Modelling shows twice-yearly HIV jab could cut new infections in South Africa by over 70% by 2045
item.description: Modelling from Wits University’s Health Economics and Epidemiology Research Office suggests that South Africa could dramatically cut new HIV infections by introducing lenacapavir (LEN), a twice-yearly...

item.title: Japan Says No Immediate Plan to Discuss Hormuz Passage With Iran Amid Tension Over Oil Routes
item.description: Japan don talk say dem no get plan for now to discuss with Iran about how Japanese oil tankers go pass the Strait of Hormuz, even though Tehran don talk say dem fit help. The matter come as tension fo...

item.title: IGP Disu To Decorate Newly Promoted Senior Police Officers Amid Internal Reshuffles
item.description: The Inspector-General of Police, Olatunji Disu, go personally decorate newly promoted Assistant Inspectors-General (AIGs) and Commissioners of Police (CPs) today, Monday, for Abuja as part of the ongo...

item.title: PM Shehbaz urges diplomacy and unity in call with Iranian president amid escalating Middle East conflict
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif held a telephone conversation with Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian at a time when the Middle East conflict has entered its fourth week, creating serious regional inst...

item.title: President Ramaphosa to Officiate Reburial of Repatriated Khoi-San Ancestral Remains in Northern Cape
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa will officiate the reburial of 63 Khoi-San ancestral human remains at the Kinderlê Monument in Steinkopf, Northern Cape, on 23 March 2026. These remains were repatriated from...

item.title: Rising costs and delays doom UK’s new nuclear power plans
item.description: The article examines the UK’s proposed new nuclear power program and argues that it is economically and practically failing. The Hinkley Point C nuclear project in Somerset, which uses two French Evol...

item.title: Tanzania Enhances Weather Alert System to Reach Remote Communities
item.description: Tanzania is working hard to ensure that vital weather information reaches everyone, particularly those living in remote villages, as the nation joins the global observance of World Meteorological Day....

item.title: Indian Army Adds Seventh Pinaka Regiment, Expands Rocket Launcher Fleet
item.description: In response to rising security threats along India's western and northern borders, especially after the 2025 conflict with Pakistan, the Indian Army has inducted its seventh Pinaka regiment. This move...

item.title: The Role of Exercise and Diet in Weight Loss: What You Need to Know
item.description: To lose weight, you need to create a calorie deficit, which can be achieved through diet, exercise, or a combination of both. While exercise can help boost calorie expenditure, it's often more effecti...

item.title: GGML HIV Campaign Gains Momentum in Moshi, Draws Thousands at Marathon Exhibition
item.description: The GGML Kili Challenge Against HIV/AIDS exhibition booth at the Kilimanjaro Marathon 2026 in Moshi has drawn a large crowd of marathon attendees and visitors, all eager to learn about the initiative’...
